qas.q: Who are the nursing members of the healthcare team?
qas.a: NA, LPN, RN, BSN, MSN

qas.q: Characteristics of an effective team.
qas.a:
comfortable and relaxed, interested and involved
open, two-way, ideas and feelings are encourage
problem solving is high

qas.q: General duties of a nurse aide.
qas.a:
perform delegated basic nursing skills i a competent, caring manner
perform delegated personal care skills in a competent , caring manner
using appropriate interpersonal skills

noisy room
lack of privacy
talking too fast
sender/reciever mentally confused

qas.a: none

Pull
Aim

sit patient up at 90 degrees
call for a nurse

qas.a: view of the world as well as a set of values, beliefs, and traditions that are handed down from generation to generation.

side rails up --> patient not being able to get up from bed

monitoring, documenting, and reporting of vital signs
management of pain as directed by nursing care plan

PRIVAVCY

an infection that a resident gets while staying or living in healthcare setting

coming in for surgery and leaving with stomach ache

qas.q: Name legally permitted duties of a NA?
qas.a: the first of two levels to precent/control infections; the basic tasks that healthcare workers must do in order to prevent and control spread of infection, whereby all body fluids, non-tact skin, and mucus membranes are treated as if they were infected

qas.q: What is a HAI (Healthcare Associated Infection)
qas.a: Handwashing

qas.q: Standard precautions are.
qas.a: having the pt in a 90 degree angle

qas.q: Most important step to prevent the threat of disease?
qas.a: cluttered room, cords on the floor, wheelchair not being locked, guard rails on bed not being in positon

qas.a:
informal, comfortable, and relaxed and members are interested and involved
open and two-way, ideas and feelings should be encouraged

sprains, dislocations, broken bones, skull fractures
bruises of face, upper arms, upper thighs, abdomen, fearfulness, paranoid,

qas.q: Define ethics.
qas.a:
performing delegating basic nursing skills in a competent, caring manner
performing delegates personal care skills in a competent, caring manner
using appropriate interpersonal skills

qas.q: List some signs and symptoms of physical abuse.
qas.a:
come in contact with blood, body fluids: urine, stool, spit, mucus, sputum,
non-intact skin: sores, cuts
mouth care, wiping nose, doing perineal care, caring for a sore, shaving resident

qas.q: Nursing Assistants are responsible for?
qas.a: must clean surface with whatever product is provided at the facility

qas.q: List examples of when you would wear gloves while performing care.
qas.a:
limited to that one part of the body
ex: infected finger

qas.q: When and generally how are contaminated work surfaces cleaned?
qas.a:
Report unsafe conditions immediately - keeping all walkways free from rugs, cords, boxes, and equipment, free of clutter
make sure call bells is withing resident reach at all times
answer call signals promptly
properly position residents in chairs and/or beds
wipe up spilled liquids immediately
pick up litter and place it in the proper container

qas.q: Examples of a localized infection?
qas.a:
use caring tone of voice and facial expression
speak slowly and distinctly, but not loud
repeat message in different ways as needed
focus on a single idea or experience
allow silence
keep massages simple and repeat often
avoid medical terms and abbreviations
pay attention to nonverbal behavior

qas.q: Steps to prevent falls include.
qas.a: reading the menu to them, talk to them

qas.q: Steps to communicate with clients from another culture.
qas.a:
did not work the wheelchair and causes patient to fall
failure to provide water or not providing care

qas.a: with each and every pt

qas.q: Which clients do we use standard precautions with?
qas.a: encourage resident to cough as forcefully as possible, ask someone to get a nurse, stay with the person, follow facility's procedure for clearing an obstructed airway.

qas.a: attitudes, different life experiences, cultural differences between sender and receiver
